concept: Recess / Bounded Pause Before an Activity Resumes
description: A bounded pause within an activity or proceeding, with an expectation that it will resume. A recess can provide time for rest, communication repair, private thought, or another stated need. 'therilu' (rest) names restorative ceasing; 'hasenu' belongs to the structure of the larger event.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'ha' recalls the held moment of 'hasi' (wait). Soft 'se' releases pressure inside the event, and final 'nu' keeps a clear edge for return.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'lona hasenu phelu.' (The meeting has a recess.) Its beginning, duration, purpose, and expected resumption can be stated in ordinary time clauses. Distinct from 'wiresu' (postpone) and an event that has ended with 'te'.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A named recess lets a group stop before speed turns confusion, overload, or fatigue into silent exclusion.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Shared work has long carried pauses shaped by heat, light, meals, prayer, and the body's need to return with steadier hands.

tags.activity: a pause held inside a larger proceeding
tags.community: shared time for rest or repair before resumption
tags.temporal: a bounded interval with an expected return
